stemHtml: One of the foremost risks associated with the use of the on-board oxygen system is that...

options.a: At high altitude and low surrounding atmospheric pressure, the debit flow of the oxygen is insufficient for normal respiration.
options.b: After maintenance, there is always a remainder of lubricant (e.g. oil or grease) in the oxygen lines, producing toxic gases.
options.c: The oxygen, in contact with oil or grease, causes a chemical reaction that can lead to spontaneous combustion.
options.d: Fungus can grow in the oxygen lines because of the high humidity of the on-board environment.
